model_note: Clustering pass run by Claude Opus 4.7 (this conversation), NOT gpt-4.1-nano. Chosen for the meta-step over gpt-4.1-nano (the per-account sweep model) to (a) avoid an OpenAI key/env dependency on the doc-side workflow and (b) leverage a stronger reasoning model on a one-shot clustering task. The per-account sweep model is unchanged.

member_specs: auto body repair and collision repair
clusters.rationale: Labour-heavy and scheduled, but predominantly in-shop (customer brings vehicle in) — limited multi-address / dispatch pattern. FSM workflow value depends on how PM frames the in-shop scheduling use case.
Same shop-based pattern as auto_body_repair. Mobile mechanics ('BryMobile Tires') exist but are the minority of the cluster.

member_specs: auto detailing and car wash services
clusters.rationale: Mobile detailing is common (on-site at customer); scheduling-heavy with recurring memberships ('Monthly Membership') visible in top_items. Labour-billed and multi-address.
Mobile windshield replacement is the dominant pattern — dispatched per-job, on-site at customer, scheduled.

Pure mobile/dispatch business — every job is a separate location, scheduling and routing are the core workflow. Billing is per-call (not recurring), but on_site_work + scheduling + labour_billing pattern is unambiguous.
Heavy on-site setup labour at customer venues; per-event scheduling; deposit + delivery patterns visible in top_items. Largest single new cluster after auto.

member_specs: Party rental equipment and event setup
Event equipment rental and event setup
Party equipment rental and event setup
clusters.rationale: Delivery + on-site setup + pickup workflow — scheduling-heavy, labour-billed, multi-address. Classic FSM pattern for chairs/tables/tents/photo booths.

On-site service is the norm but billing is more product-shaped (per-menu / per-head) than labour-shaped. PM call on whether the FSM upsell makes sense — catering software is its own competitive category.
Textbook FSM trade — on-site install/repair, scheduling, labour-billed, recurring service relationships. Top_items show parts SKUs + trip charges.

example_business_names: JC Movers & Senior Transitions Of SWFL
clusters.rationale: Pure on-site labour-billed multi-address work. Hourly billing + truck fees visible in top_items.

member_specs: window and door installation and repair
Window and door installation and repair
clusters.rationale: On-site install at customer property — same FSM profile as flooring/roofing already in the enum.
On-site labour trade — slots naturally between painting and renovations in the existing enum.
On-site exterior install/maintenance — peer trade to roofing, often run alongside it.
On-site install + measurement + materials labour. Could plausibly fold into a broader exterior_install bucket if PM wants fewer ids.

member_specs: solar energy system installation and components
clusters.rationale: On-site install — large multi-day projects, materials + labour mix. Could fold into electrical, but solar businesses are usually marketed as their own vertical.
Mobile on-site exterior cleaning — recurring (often quarterly/seasonal) and route-scheduled. Sits adjacent to existing cleaning industry.

member_specs: Dog walking and pet sitting services
clusters.rationale: Highly recurring + multi-address scheduling — Single Walk / Double walk / Pet sitting visible in top_items. Different vertical from rest of FSM enum but strong scheduling fit.
Shop fabrication + on-site measurement + on-site install. The install half of the workflow is FSM-shaped.
Mostly shop-based fab; some mobile welding ('Mobile Welding') exists but visible top_items lean shop. Could collapse into other if PM thinks the in-shop pattern dominates.
Non-auto glass (shower, storefront, residential). On-site measurement + install — top_items include 'LABOR (PER WINDOW)' and 'SHOWER GLASS'.
Multi-property recurring maintenance + trip-based service calls — top_items show 'Management Fees', 'Trips to home', 'Eviction'. Scheduling-heavy across many addresses.

unclustered.note: Freight/courier — not customer-facing FSM. Keep in other.
Rental fleet — not FSM. Keep in other.
Mostly in-shop custom install — borderline FSM, too small alone to justify its own id. Keep in other.
Creative service, not classic FSM scheduling/labour pattern. Keep in other.

summary.note: At threshold>=5 the proposal moves ~14% of strong+other accounts (791/5544) into named ids. The remaining ~85% live in the long tail (count<5 specs); their individual cluster signal is too weak to justify enum entries today.
